goals: Render GLSL effects on the server using ModernGL headless (no display required).
Return all rendered frames as a single IMAGE batch tensor `[N, H, W, C]`.
Expose duration and FPS as configurable inputs on the node.

userStories.title: GLSL Frame Rendering
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want the node to render each video frame by running the GLSL shader on my input image server-side so that the output frames match the preview I saw in the selector node.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The node creates a ModernGL offscreen context with `moderngl.create_standalone_context()` (no display dependency).
For each frame `i` in `[0, round(duration * fps))`, `u_time` is set to `i / fps`.
`u_image` is bound to the input image as an OpenGL texture.
`u_resolution` is set to the input image dimensions `(width, height)`.
The node raises a descriptive `ValueError` if the shader named by `effect_name`
The ModernGL context and all GL resources are released (`ctx.release()`) after rendering,

userStories.title: IMAGE Batch Output
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI workflow builder, I want the node to output an IMAGE batch so that I can connect it directly to any downstream node — VHS Save, AnimateDiff, image processors — without format conversion.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Each rendered frame is read back from the ModernGL framebuffer as raw RGB bytes.
Frames are converted to a `torch.Tensor` of shape `[N, H, W, 3]` with dtype `float32`
The output tensor is returned as the node's `IMAGE` output.
For an input of shape `[1, H, W, C]`, a duration of 3 s at 30 fps, the output shape
Connecting the output to a ComfyUI `PreviewImage` node displays all frames as a

userStories.title: Duration and FPS Configuration
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want to configure the animation duration and frame rate directly on the node so that I can generate a 3-second batch or a 10-second batch without editing code.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The node exposes an `INT` input `fps` with default 30, min 1, max 60.
The node exposes a `FLOAT` input `duration` with default 3.0, min 0.5, max 60.0, step 0.5.
Total frames rendered equals `round(duration * fps)`.
Both inputs are visible and adjustable as standard ComfyUI node widgets.

userStories.title: ComfyUI Node Integration
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI workflow builder, I want the Video Generator Node to accept IMAGE + EFFECT_NAME inputs and return an IMAGE batch so that I can connect it directly to the Effect Selector Node and any downstream image node.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The node is registered in `NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS` as `"CoolVideoGenerator"`.
The node declares inputs: `image` (IMAGE), `effect_name` (STRING), `fps` (INT), `duration` (FLOAT).
The node output type is `("IMAGE",)`.
The node appears in the ComfyUI node browser under the category `"CoolEffects"`.
The node completes rendering a 512×512 image, 3 s at 30 fps (90 frames) in under

functionalRequirements.description: The Python node class file is `nodes/video_generator.py`.
Shader source is loaded exclusively via `shaders/loader.py::load_shader(effect_name)` — no inline GLSL strings in the node.
The ModernGL context must be created with `moderngl.create_standalone_context()`.
A full-screen quad (two triangles covering NDC `[-1, 1]`) is used as the render geometry.
Frames are read back via `fbo.read(components=3)` and converted to numpy arrays before stacking into a torch tensor.
The final tensor must be stacked with `torch.stack` or `torch.from_numpy` into shape `[N, H, W, 3]`, dtype `float32`, range `[0, 1]`.
All GL resources (context, texture, program, fbo, vbo) must be released in a `finally` block.
